perspectives.label: Java Layers
perspectives.description: Enforces the layered architecture of the loan management application

groups.label: controller
groups.description: REST and web controllers — entry point for all HTTP requests

groups.label: service
groups.description: Business logic layer — orchestrates domain operations

groups.label: repository
groups.description: Data access layer — JPA repositories only

groups.label: model
groups.description: Domain entities — pure JPA objects with no layer dependencies

groups.label: dto
groups.description: Data transfer objects — plain data carriers for transport

groups.label: mapper
groups.description: MapStruct mappers — translate between model and dto only

groups.label: config
groups.description: Spring configuration classes — wires beans, no controller coupling

constraints.description: Service must never call back into controller — prevents reverse dependency cycle
Repository must never call service — data layer must remain unaware of business logic
Repository must never call controller — no upward dependency from data layer
Model (entities) must not depend on controller — keeps domain objects pure
Model must not depend on service layer
Model must not depend on repository — avoids circular JPA references
DTOs are plain data carriers — must not depend on service
DTOs must not depend on controller layer
DTOs must not depend on repository — no data access from transport objects
Mappers translate between model and dto only — must not call service logic
Mappers must not depend on controller
Mappers must not call repositories directly
Controller may only call service — direct repository access bypasses business logic
Controller must not import model entities directly — use DTOs for transport
Config may wire service beans but must not depend on controller — avoids circular config
